root_text.an4.234:1.1: Atha kho sikhāmoggallāno brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an4.234:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho sikhāmoggallāno brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.234:2.1: “Purimāni, bho gotama, divasāni purimatarāni soṇakāyano māṇavo yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.234:2.2: ‘samaṇo gotamo sabbakammānaṁ akiriyaṁ paññapeti, sabbakammānaṁ kho pana akiriyaṁ paññapento ucchedaṁ āha lokassa’—
root_text.an4.234:3.1: “Dassanampi kho ahaṁ, brāhmaṇa, soṇakāyanassa māṇavassa nābhijānāmi;
root_text.an4.234:3.3: Cattārimāni, brāhmaṇa, kammāni mayā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā paveditāni.
root_text.an4.234:3.8: atthi, brāhmaṇa, kammaṁ akaṇhaasukkaṁ akaṇhaasukkavipākaṁ kammakkhayāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an4.234:4.2: Idha, brāhmaṇa, ekacco sabyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, sabyābajjhaṁ vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, sabyābajjhaṁ manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti.
root_text.an4.234:4.3: So sabyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, sabyābajjhaṁ vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, sabyābajjhaṁ manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā sabyābajjhaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.234:4.4: Tamenaṁ sabyābajjhaṁ lokaṁ upapannaṁ samānaṁ sabyābajjhā phassā phusanti.
root_text.an4.234:4.5: So sabyābajjhehi phassehi phuṭṭho samāno sabyābajjhaṁ vedanaṁ vediyati ekantadukkhaṁ, seyyathāpi sattā nerayikā.
root_text.an4.234:4.6: Idaṁ vuccati, brāhmaṇa, kammaṁ kaṇhaṁ kaṇhavipākaṁ.
root_text.an4.234:5.2: Idha, brāhmaṇa, ekacco abyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, abyābajjhaṁ vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, abyābajjhaṁ manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti.
root_text.an4.234:5.3: So abyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, abyābajjhaṁ vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, abyābajjhaṁ manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā abyābajjhaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.234:5.4: Tamenaṁ abyābajjhaṁ lokaṁ upapannaṁ samānaṁ abyābajjhā phassā phusanti.
root_text.an4.234:5.5: So abyābajjhehi phassehi phuṭṭho samāno abyābajjhaṁ vedanaṁ vediyati ekantasukhaṁ, seyyathāpi devā subhakiṇhā.
root_text.an4.234:5.6: Idaṁ vuccati, brāhmaṇa, kammaṁ sukkaṁ sukkavipākaṁ.
root_text.an4.234:6.2: Idha, brāhmaṇa, ekacco sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti.
root_text.an4.234:6.3: So sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.234:6.4: Tamenaṁ sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi lokaṁ upapannaṁ samānaṁ sabyābajjhāpi abyābajjhāpi phassā phusanti.
root_text.an4.234:6.5: So sabyābajjhehipi abyābajjhehipi phassehi phuṭṭho samāno sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi vedanaṁ vediyati vokiṇṇasukhadukkhaṁ, seyyathāpi manussā ekacce ca devā ekacce ca vinipātikā.
root_text.an4.234:6.6: Idaṁ vuccati, brāhmaṇa, kammaṁ kaṇhasukkaṁ kaṇhasukkavipākaṁ.
root_text.an4.234:7.1: Katamañca, brāhmaṇa, kammaṁ akaṇhaasukkaṁ akaṇhaasukkavipākaṁ kammakkhayāya saṁvattati?
root_text.an4.234:7.2: Tatra, brāhmaṇa, yamidaṁ kammaṁ kaṇhaṁ kaṇhavipākaṁ tassa pahānāya yā cetanā, yamidaṁ kammaṁ sukkaṁ sukkavipākaṁ tassa pahānāya yā cetanā, yamidaṁ kammaṁ kaṇhasukkaṁ kaṇhasukkavipākaṁ tassa pahānāya yā cetanā—
root_text.an4.234:7.3: idaṁ vuccati, brāhmaṇa, kammaṁ akaṇhaasukkaṁ akaṇhaasukkavipākaṁ kammakkhayāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an4.234:7.4: Imāni kho, brāhmaṇa, cattāri kammāni mayā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā paveditānī”ti.

translation_text.an4.234:1.1: Then Sikhāmoggallāna the brahmin went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an4.234:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, Sikhāmoggallāna sat down to one side, and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an4.234:2.1: “Worthy Gotama, a few days ago the student Soṇakāyana came to me and said:
translation_text.an4.234:2.2: ‘The ascetic Gotama advocates not doing any deeds. So he teaches the annihilation of the world!’
translation_text.an4.234:2.3: The world exists through deeds, and it remains because deeds are undertaken.”
translation_text.an4.234:3.1: “Brahmin, I can’t recall even seeing the student Soṇakāyana,
translation_text.an4.234:3.2: so how could we possibly have had such a discussion?
translation_text.an4.234:3.3: I declare these four kinds of deeds, having realized them with my own insight.
translation_text.an4.234:3.5: There are dark deeds with dark results;
translation_text.an4.234:3.7: dark and bright deeds with dark and bright results; and
translation_text.an4.234:3.8: neither dark nor bright deeds with neither dark nor bright results, which lead to the ending of deeds.
translation_text.an4.234:4.1: And what are the dark deeds with dark results?
translation_text.an4.234:4.2: It’s when someone makes hurtful choices by way of body, speech, and mind. …
translation_text.an4.234:4.5: Touched by hurtful contacts, they experience hurtful feelings that are exclusively painful—like the beings in hell.
translation_text.an4.234:4.6: These are called dark deeds with dark results.
translation_text.an4.234:5.1: And what are bright deeds with bright results?
translation_text.an4.234:5.2: It’s when someone makes pleasing choices by way of body, speech, and mind. …
translation_text.an4.234:5.5: Touched by pleasing contacts, they experience pleasing feelings of perfect happiness—like the gods of universal beauty.
translation_text.an4.234:5.6: These are called bright deeds with bright results.
translation_text.an4.234:6.1: And what are dark and bright deeds with dark and bright results?
translation_text.an4.234:6.2: It’s when someone makes both hurtful and pleasing choices by way of body, speech, and mind. …
translation_text.an4.234:6.5: Touched by both hurtful and pleasing contacts, they experience both hurtful and pleasing feelings that are a mixture of pleasure and pain—like humans, some gods, and some beings in the underworld.
translation_text.an4.234:6.6: These are called dark and bright deeds with dark and bright results.
translation_text.an4.234:7.1: And what are neither dark nor bright deeds with neither dark nor bright results, which lead to the ending of deeds?
translation_text.an4.234:7.2: It’s the intention to give up dark deeds with dark results, bright deeds with bright results, and both dark and bright deeds with both dark and bright results.
translation_text.an4.234:7.3: These are called neither dark nor bright deeds with neither dark nor bright results, which lead to the ending of deeds.
translation_text.an4.234:7.4: These are the four kinds of deeds that I declare, having realized them with my own insight.”
